title: ScrollView and Lists
description: Create scrollable content and render dynamic lists using ScrollView and mapping.

slides.title: ScrollView 📜
slides.content:
# `<ScrollView>`

The ScrollView component is a generic scrolling container that can host multiple components and views. It's useful when you have a small number of items that don't change, or when you need to scroll to a specific child.

```jsx
import { ScrollView, Text } from 'react-native';

<ScrollView>
  <Text>Item 1</Text>
  <Text>Item 2</Text>
  <Text>Item 3</Text>
  {/* ... many more items */}
</ScrollView>
```

ScrollView renders all its children at once, so it's **not efficient for long lists** (use FlatList for that).

slides.title: Rendering Dynamic Lists 🔁
slides.content:
## Using `map()` to render an array

Often you have an array of data and you want to render a list of components. Use the JavaScript `map` function:

```jsx
const names = ['Alice', 'Bob', 'Charlie'];

<ScrollView>
  {names.map((name, index) => (
    <Text key={index}>{name}</Text>
  ))}
</ScrollView>
```

⚠️ **Always provide a `key` prop** when rendering lists. It helps React identify which items have changed. Ideally use a unique ID from your data; otherwise use the index as a fallback (but this can cause issues if the list order changes).

quizData.question: Why is it important to provide a `key` prop when rendering a list of components?

options.text: To help React identify which items changed
To make the list scroll faster

quizData.explanation: Keys give React a way to track elements for efficient updates and re-renders.

slides.title: Horizontal Scrolling ↔️
slides.content:
## Make it horizontal

Set `horizontal={true}` to enable horizontal scrolling.

```jsx
<ScrollView horizontal>
  <View style={{ width: 100, height: 100, backgroundColor: 'red' }} />
  <View style={{ width: 100, height: 100, backgroundColor: 'green' }} />
  <View style={{ width: 100, height: 100, backgroundColor: 'blue' }} />
</ScrollView>
```

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content: If your list is very long (more than a few hundred items), switch to **FlatList** or **SectionList**. They render only the visible items, saving memory and improving performance.
